name: Sample Player
description: This algorithm plays samples from the MicroSD card. It is loosely based on the “SD 6 Triggers” algorithm on the disting EX. Whereas the “Poly Multisample” algorithm is aimed more at playing pitched instruments, this algorithm is mainly designed for playing simple one-shot samples e.g. drums. It supports both velocity switches and round robins per sample. The trigger inputs are velocity sensitive – the voltage of the gate signal is used like the velocity of a MIDI note. 5V corresponds to maximum velocity.
gui_description: The display shows the MIDI note names of the samples being played by the various triggers, superimposed on a bar indicating the trigger’s envelope. The folder and filename of the most recently triggered sample is also shown.

specifications.name: Triggers
specifications.description: The number of individual sample triggers.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: An overall gain to apply in addition to the per-trigger gain.

parameters.name: Round robin mode
parameters.description: The round-robin mode.

parameters.name: Folder
parameters.description: Sets the folder from which to choose a sample.

parameters.name: Sample
parameters.description: Sets the sample within the folder.

parameters.name: Transpose
parameters.description: Sets the sample tuning in semitones.

parameters.name: Fine tune
parameters.description: Sets the sample fine tuning.

parameters.name: Gain
parameters.description: The output level.

parameters.name: Pan
parameters.description: The stereo pan position.

parameters.name: Vel(ocity) depth
parameters.description: Sets the amount by which the velocity affects the playback level.

parameters.name: Choke group
parameters.description: The voice's “choke group”. When a voice in a choke group is triggered, it ends the playback of any other voices in the same choke group.

parameters.name: Play
parameters.description: Manually triggers sample playback.

parameters.name: Velocity
parameters.description: The velocity to use when ‘Play’ triggers playback.

parameters.name: Loop
parameters.description: Sets whether the sample will loop. ‘From WAV file’ will loop if the sample has loop points defined in the file and not otherwise. ‘Off’ and ‘On’ force the sample to loop or not.

parameters.name: MIDI channel
parameters.description: The MIDI channel to respond to.

parameters.name: MIDI note
parameters.description: The MIDI note to respond to, or “Any”.

parameters.name: I2C channel
parameters.description: Sets the I2C channel.

parameters.name: Envelope
parameters.description: Enables an ADSR volume envelope. If the envelope is disabled, the sample simply plays once until the end.

parameters.name: Attack
parameters.description: Sets the envelope attack time.

parameters.name: Decay
parameters.description: Sets the envelope decay time.

parameters.name: Sustain
parameters.description: Sets the envelope sustain level.

parameters.name: Release
parameters.description: Sets the envelope release time.

parameters.name: Confirm change
parameters.description: Sets whether the folder and/or sample changes immediately or requires confirmation.

parameters.name: Retrigger on change
parameters.description: If enabled, changing the sample selection while the sample is playing retriggers the sample.

parameters.name: Restart on change
parameters.description: Chooses where to start playback from if the sample is retriggered as a result of ‘Retrigger on change’. If set, the sample restarts from the beginning; otherwise, the playback position within the sample is preserved.

parameters.name: Start trim coarse
parameters.description: Sets an amount to trim off the start, as a percentage of the sample length.

parameters.name: Start trim fine
parameters.description: Adjusts the amount to trim off the start, in sample frames.

parameters.name: End trim coarse
parameters.description: Sets an amount to trim off the end, as a percentage of the sample length.

parameters.name: End trim fine
parameters.description: Adjusts the amount to trim off the end, in sample frames.

parameters.name: Offset type
parameters.description: Sets how the start offset will be determined.

parameters.name: Offset ms
parameters.description: The start offset, if the type is ‘Milliseconds’.

parameters.name: Offset %
parameters.description: The start offset, if the type is ‘Percent’.

parameters.name: Offset div
parameters.description: The start offset divisor, if the type is ‘Division’.

parameters.name: Offset mult
parameters.description: The start offset multiplier, if the type is ‘Division’.

parameters.name: Record
parameters.description: Set to ‘On’ to activate recording and ‘Off’ to stop recording.

parameters.name: Record input L
parameters.description: The bus to use for the left, or mono, audio input.

parameters.name: Record input R
parameters.description: The bus to use for the right audio input.

parameters.name: Record gain
parameters.description: A gain to apply when recording.

parameters.name: Use recording
parameters.description: Set to ‘On’ to use the recording instead of a sample on the MicroSD card. Ignored if there is no recording.

parameters.name: Save to folder
parameters.description: Sets the folder name in which to save the sample to the MicroSD card.

parameters.name: Save as
parameters.description: Sets the base file name to use when saving the sample to the MicroSD card.

parameters.name: Output mode
parameters.description: The standard Add/Replace mode selector.

input_ports.name: Trigger input
input_ports.description: Velocity sensitive gate signal used to trigger sample playback.

input_ports.name: Record input L
input_ports.description: Left or mono audio input for sampling.

input_ports.name: Record input R
input_ports.description: Right audio input for stereo sampling.

input_ports.name: Trigger input
input_ports.description: The input bus to use as the gate/trigger.
